summary: Cloudflare Workers outbound fetch() blocks raw IP addresses (error 1003) and strips non-standard ports → use a hostname via DNS record. Port must be in Cloudflare's supported list (80, 443, 8080, 8443, etc). Same-zone grey-cloud DNS records work for non-standard ports.

Cloudflare Workers module-level singletons persist across requests in the same isolate but env bindings differ per request → never cache clients that depend on env vars at module level. Create fresh per request.
Cloudflare Workers in-memory caching (Map, global variables) is useless — each request may run in a different isolate. Use KV or Cache API for persistence across requests.

@supabase/supabase-js assumes Supabase-specific URL paths (/rest/v1/) → for raw PostgREST, use @supabase/postgrest-js directly. Same query API (.from(), .rpc(), .select()) without the path assumptions.
PostgREST role switching requires GRANT target_role TO authenticator_role. Without it, every request fails with 'permission denied to set role'. Easy to miss during setup.
PostgREST connection string with special characters in password breaks parsing (password chars interpreted as port/host) → use hex-only passwords (openssl rand -hex 32) or URL-encode the password.
OAuth state parameter must be cryptographically signed (HMAC-SHA256), not just base64 encoded. Plain base64 lets attackers craft arbitrary redirect URIs and steal tokens.
Zod v4 z.record() requires two arguments: z.record(keySchema, valueSchema). Zod v3 accepted one argument. Upgrading silently breaks record schemas at compile time.
Cloudflare Workers AI bge-small-en-v1.5 produces 384-dimension embeddings (not 1536 like OpenAI). pgvector column must be vector(384). Using wrong dimension silently produces bad search results.
Hetzner Cloud Firewall is separate from OS-level ufw. Both must be configured. Hetzner firewall filters before traffic reaches the server (better), ufw filters at the OS (backup layer).
Flutter dart:io Platform.isAndroid breaks web builds — dart:io not available on web. Use defaultTargetPlatform from foundation.dart which is web-safe.
Flutter Drift .customStatement() for DELETE doesn't trigger stream cache invalidation — active watchAll() streams won't update. Use .customUpdate() or Drift's typed delete methods instead.
Flutter Drift table named 'Movies' generates row class 'Movy', 'Series' generates 'Sery'. Drift uses English singularization rules. All queries and imports must use the generated names.
Flutter Drift import conflicts with test matchers — must import with 'hide isNotNull, isNull' to avoid ambiguity with test framework matchers.
Flutter Drift import conflicts with Flutter Column widget — must import with 'hide Column' when used alongside Flutter's Column in the same file.
Android PopScope broken on Android 14+ (targetSdk 34+) — default OnBackInvokedCallback overrides Flutter's PopScope. Fix: set android:enableOnBackInvokedCallback="false" in AndroidManifest.xml.

VLC player events (isPlaying, playingState) unreliable for IPTV live streams — false stall detection, error overlays while audio plays. Use position advancement as ground truth instead of state events.
Flutter HeroController shared across multiple Navigators causes animation conflicts. Wrap nested Navigators in HeroControllerScope.none to isolate hero animations.
Flutter Riverpod: watching filtered providers for category counts returns counts that change with search query. Watch unfiltered source providers for true totals.
Flutter TTS rapid button taps drop words due to queue race condition → implement async Queue with sequential processing. Also stop/clear queue on sentence clear.
Flutter .cast<>() on JSON arrays is unsafe — panics on type mismatch. Use .whereType<>() for safe filtering that skips non-matching elements.
Flutter immersive mode resets on app resume (Android). Must re-apply SystemChrome settings in AppLifecycleListener resume callback.
PostgreSQL shared_buffers on shared VPS — don't allocate 1GB on a 4GB box running other services. 512MB caches a <100MB database entirely. Over-allocation starves other processes.
PostgreSQL default random_page_cost (4.0) assumes spinning disk. On SSD set to 1.1 with effective_io_concurrency=200. Wrong settings cause query planner to avoid index scans.
SvelteKit SPA on Cloudflare Pages — hard refresh on /route causes 404 instead of SPA shell. Add _redirects file: '/* /index.html 200' or use adapter-static with fallback.
JavaScript bitwise operations default to signed 32-bit. Flutter uses unsigned 32-bit ARGB for colors. Hex-to-color conversion must use unsigned right shift (>>> 0) to match.
Godot 4.x Android plugin discovery requires <meta-data> tag in AndroidManifest.xml. Without it, plugin code is bundled but Engine.get_singleton() returns null at runtime.

Godot exports with 'Apple Distribution' code signing but Xcode debug builds expect 'Apple Development' → code signing conflict after every export. Workaround: sed -i to replace in pbxproj after export.
Godot regenerates exports/ios/ on each export, wiping custom Podfile. Keep master Podfile elsewhere, copy before pod install.
Firebase GoogleService-Info.plist must be added to Xcode project AFTER pod install, not before. Wrong order causes Firebase initialization failures.
Google Play Billing Library 8.0+ requires queryProductDetails() before purchase(). Without it, purchase returns DEVELOPER_ERROR (code 5). Cache product details on billing connection.
Godot ConfigFile.get_value() returns untyped Array — cannot assign to Array[String] in GDScript. Silently fails. Must explicitly cast: result as Array[String].

Android Firebase dependencies in Godot plugins must be declared in TWO places: plugin's build.gradle.kts with compileOnly(), AND Godot export plugin's _get_android_dependencies(). Missing either causes runtime crash.
Go state cache TOCTOU race: multiple RLock acquisitions when reading cache allows state to change between reads. Use single lock acquisition per read operation.
DateTime stored as local wall clock but compared with .toUtc() causes double-conversion (1-2h offset). If times are already local, compare local-to-local without timezone conversion.
Cloudflare Pages SPA: add _headers file to prevent HTML caching while keeping immutable hashed assets cached forever. Without it, users get stale HTML after deploys.
SQLite LIKE queries with backslash escaping silently fail without ESCAPE clause. Must add ESCAPE '\' to every LIKE that uses backslash-escaped wildcards.
iOS Network Extensions (VPN, content filters) do not work in iOS Simulator — requires physical device for testing. No workaround.
GDPR consent must be propagated to ALL mediation networks (Unity Ads, etc), not just the primary ad SDK. Missing propagation causes individual consent dialogs to appear.
Flutter animation callback .then() can fire after widget dispose → crash. Always add mounted check (if (mounted)) before reversing or modifying state in animation callbacks.
API rate limiting: never trust Content-Length header for payload size validation. Check actual body size after reading. Content-Length can be spoofed or missing.
NOT IN subquery in PostgreSQL is less efficient than NOT EXISTS for large tables. NOT EXISTS can use anti-join optimization and stop at first match. Use NOT EXISTS for exclusion queries.
Flutter Riverpod v2 provider state persists across builds — must explicitly reset nav stack, caches, and dependent state when switching contexts (profiles, boards, imports).
MCP tool descriptions are the ONLY way LLMs know when to call tools. Include: when to call, when NOT to call, what it returns, and an example. Max ~1024 chars for Claude compatibility.
MCP tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint) help clients decide whether to auto-approve. Set them — even though clients don't all use them yet, it's forward-compatible.
Fastlane skip_upload_metadata must be true for Google Play uploads — false risks overwriting manually managed store listing metadata with stale local data.
Flutter share_plus v10.x API changed: use Share.shareXFiles() static method, not SharePlus.instance. Breaking change not obvious from error messages.
pgvector HNSW index only works with specific distance operators. vector_cosine_ops for cosine similarity (<=>), vector_l2_ops for L2 distance (<->). Wrong operator ignores the index.
